 {"id":"bd-24g4","title":"fix: markup hyperlink state restoration bug with interleaved tags","description":"Fixed bug in pop_style() where link URLs were incorrectly restored when closing non-link tags that were opened inside a link. Example: [link=a][bold]text[/link]more[/bold]after - 'after' incorrectly got the link. Root cause: entry.link_url captured at push time became stale when link closed. Fix: derive link state from remaining stack entries instead of restoring from popped entry.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:18:34.512860386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T18:18:40.069318957Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T18:18:40.069298097Z","close_reason":"Fixed: Changed pop_style() to derive link state from remaining stack entries instead of restoring potentially stale entry.link_url. Added test parse_link_with_nested_tag_outlasting_link. All tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-24li","title":"Add ANSI sequence parser for HeadlessTerm","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:23:37.962135466Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:57:57.939511371Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:57:57.939391445Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24li","depends_on_id":"bd-1tz6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-255jd","title":"Parser: HTS/TBC/CBT tab stop sequences + DECKPAM/DECKPNM keypad modes + conformance fixtures","description":"## Gap Analysis

frankenterm-core cursor.rs has full tab stop logic (set_tab_stop, clear_tab_stop, clear_all_tab_stops, next_tab_stop, prev_tab_stop) but the parser has NO action variants for:
- HTS (ESC H) - Set Tab Stop at current column
- TBC (CSI Ps g) - Tab Clear (0=at cursor, 3=all)
- CBT (CSI Ps Z) - Cursor Backward Tabulation
- DECKPAM (ESC =) - Application Keypad Mode
- DECKPNM (ESC >) - Normal Keypad Mode

## Deliverables
1. Add Action variants: SetTabStop, ClearTabStop(u16), BackTab(u16), ApplicationKeypad, NormalKeypad
2. Wire parser to emit these from ESC and CSI decoders
3. Add conformance fixtures for tab stop manipulation and backward tab
4. Extend differential_terminal.rs to apply these actions
5. Add tests for all new sequences

## Acceptance Criteria
- Parser emits correct actions for ESC H, CSI g, CSI Z, ESC =, ESC >
- Conformance fixtures pass for tab operations (set/clear/backward)
- cargo check, clippy, fmt all pass
- Existing 71 parser tests + new tests all green","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T02:05:24.375944945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T02:05:37.343202553Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-255jd","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T02:05:37.343149905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- TBC (CSI Ps g) - Tab Clear (0=at cursor, 3=all)
- CBT (CSI Ps Z) - Cursor Backward Tabulation
- DECKPAM (ESC =) - Application Keypad Mode
- DECKPNM (ESC >) - Normal Keypad Mode

## Deliverables
1. Add Action variants: SetTabStop, ClearTabStop(u16), BackTab(u16), ApplicationKeypad, NormalKeypad
2. Wire parser to emit these from ESC and CSI decoders

4. Extend differential_terminal.rs to apply these actions

- cargo check, clippy, fmt all pass
- Existing 71 parser tests + new tests all green","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyDeer","created_at":"2026-02-09T02:05:24.375944945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T02:15:03.772622200Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T02:15:03.772600440Z","close_reason":"Completed: parser HTS/TBC/CBT + keypad actions are wired, conformance fixtures exist, and differential/proptest harnesses now apply the new actions; all gates green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-255jd","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T02:05:37.343149905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-25df9","title":"Mermaid Link/Click Rendering + Hyperlink Policy","description":"Background
- Mermaid supports click/link directives and URLs in nodes/edges.
- Terminal rendering must present links safely and deterministically with graceful fallbacks.

Scope
- Define link policy: allow/deny, sanitize URLs, and map to terminal hyperlink sequences if supported.
- Expose config options: enable_links, link_mode (inline/footnote/off), sanitize_mode (strict/lenient).
- Provide fallback rendering: inline link markers + footnote legend or tooltip panel (legend layout via bd-1oa1y).
- Support linkStyle directives where feasible (underline/accent) in terminal constraints.
- Log link_count, link_mode, and sanitization outcomes to JSONL.

Considerations
- Security: no external fetches, sanitize URLs, and prevent escape injection.
- Deterministic output across capability profiles; same input yields same link_mode.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for link parsing, sanitization, and policy decisions.
- Snapshot tests for hyperlink-capable and ASCII fallback modes (inline/footnote).
- PTY E2E logs include link_count and link_mode.

Success Criteria
- Links render safely and consistently; unsupported modes degrade predictably.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlueOtter","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:14:06.991818179Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:39:06.780070987Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T06:39:06.779969245Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-25df9","depends_on_id":"bd-1ri6i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:14:13.525712086Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25df9","depends_on_id":"bd-315zw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:14:13.983347276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25df9","depends_on_id":"bd-3monr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:14:13.759637186Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25df9","depends_on_id":"bd-j20xh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:14:14.211052076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25df9","depends_on_id":"bd-pt0dv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:14:13.293606691Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Results inform whether we need additional optimizations for layered composition.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:30:53.625827734Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T07:22:42.124165185Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T07:22:42.124145398Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p","title":"FrankenTerm.WASM: full custom terminal stack (replace crossterm + xterm.js)","description":"Vision
- Build FrankenTerm: a first-party, Rust-first terminal engine and renderer that runs natively and as `frankenterm.wasm`.
- Replace BOTH Crossterm (native) and xterm.js (web) so we control correctness, latency, and UX end-to-end.

North-Star Outcomes
- Web: `ftui-demo-showcase` runs fully client-side inside FrankenTerm at interactive framerates and feels like a native app.
- Web (terminal-class): FrankenTerm can connect to a real PTY bridge and run scripted remote sessions (bidirectional protocol, not just rendering).
- Native: a first-party backend with better lifecycle semantics + better perf than Crossterm.

Hard Constraints (Non-negotiable)
- Safe Rust in-tree (`#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` in our crates; deps may use unsafe internally).
- Determinism where requested: seeded demos, explicit time sources, record/replay.
- Extreme Optimization discipline: baseline/profile/prove/one-lever/gates.
- Accessibility: keyboard-only usability + screen-reader strategy (even with WebGPU/canvas rendering).
- Security for remote mode: explicit threat model, origin/auth controls, no accidental command-exec in prod.

Success Criteria
- Builds:
  - `cargo test` green for workspace
  - `cargo check --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` succeeds for web crates
- Correctness:
  - Golden trace replay gates are green (native + web)
  - Native PTY E2E suite (`tests/e2e/scripts/*`) stays green with detailed JSONL logs
- UX:
  - Web terminal supports: selection+copy/paste, scrollback, OSC 8 hyperlinks, resize storms, search, and a11y modes
- Performance:
  - 120x40 steady-state rendering at 60fps on modern hardware for representative workloads
  - Keystroke-to-photon latency budgets defined and measured for remote sessions
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:48:34.961095168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:15:55.086771192Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

- Terminal data model (cells/lines/scrollback/selection) + parser/engine for VT/ANSI output streams.
- Designed so ftui can render directly (cell-buffer feed) and FrankenTerm can optionally accept ANSI streams for generality.

Deliverables
- New `frankenterm-core` crate: Grid, Scrollback, Selection, Cursor, Modes, Parser, Patch API.
- Unit + proptest coverage.
- Bench harnesses for parser throughput + grid ops.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","title":"Spec: FrankenTerm VT/ANSI support matrix + conformance fixtures","description":"Goal
- Define exactly what FrankenTerm will implement (and what it will intentionally NOT implement) for VT/ANSI output streams.

Deliverables
- Support matrix for: C0/C1 controls, CSI sequences, SGR, OSC (incl OSC 8), DEC private modes, cursor modes, scrolling, insert/delete, tabs.
- A conformance fixture corpus (byte streams + expected grid snapshots) with deterministic replay.
- Clear "compat" boundaries: which behaviors must match de-facto terminals.

Acceptance Criteria
- Parser work can be driven directly from this matrix and fixtures.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:27.823474788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:39:49.449662450Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:39:49.449643043Z","close_reason":"Wrote comprehensive VT/ANSI support matrix at docs/spec/frankenterm-vt-support-matrix.md covering: C0/C1 controls, CSI (cursor, erase, insert/delete, scroll, modes), SGR (attributes, 16/256/RGB colors), OSC (title, hyperlinks, clipboard), DEC private modes, Kitty keyboard protocol, character sets. Includes 15 sections, explicit priority levels (Must/Should/Won't), parser state machine spec, conformance fixture format with 6 initial fixtures, and CI integration plan.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:27.823474788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:27.823474788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:27.823474788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.10","title":"Bench: parser throughput + patch apply cost (core engine)","description":"Goal
- Build repeatable benchmarks for the core engine so optimization work is rigorous and gated.

Bench Corpus
- Maintain a realistic byte-stream corpus (build logs, dense SGR, markdown-ish output, unicode-heavy lines).
- Every benchmark run records corpus id/hash + git_sha so results are comparable.

Metrics
- Parser throughput (MB/s) for each corpus stream.
- Patch generation + patch apply cost under typical workloads.
- Memory footprint per 1k lines scrollback and under resize storms.

Outputs
- Machine-readable results (JSON) suitable for CI regression gates.

Acceptance Criteria
- Variance is characterized and documented; thresholds are set for meaningful regressions (p95 or distributional tests).
","notes":"2026-02-09 RainyMill follow-up: added scrollback_memory and resize_storm benchmark groups to parser_patch_bench, emitted machine-readable JSON events for memory/resize metrics, expanded bench_budget parser mappings and budgets for parser_throughput_large/full_pipeline/patch_diff_apply/resize_storm/scrollback_memory, and validated with cargo check/clippy/fmt plus ./scripts/bench_budget.sh --quick --json.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RainyMill","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:20.414665310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T02:06:02.018299494Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:57:59.666788056Z","close_reason":"Bench landed in 4cabfd1d; bd-lff4p.1.6 is closed; verified with cargo check -p frankenterm-core --benches","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:20.414665310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:20.414665310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:20.414665310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:20.414665310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.10","title":"Optimize: ftui-tty reduce poll syscalls during burst input","description":"Goal
- Improve ftui-tty input throughput under bursty workloads (pipes/PTYs, paste storms) without changing event semantics.

Baseline (release)
- hyperfine (test binary): tests::pipe_large_ascii_burst_roundtrips mean ~123.9ms
- strace -c: ~4098 poll + ~4122 read syscalls

Change
- In read_event(), when the event queue is empty and the reader is nonblocking, opportunistically drain any newly-arrived bytes (nonblocking) before returning None.

After (release)
- hyperfine (test binary): mean ~101.9ms (1.22x faster)
- strace -c: ~2 poll + ~539 read syscalls

Isomorphism proof
- Ordering preserved: yes (bytes parsed in-order; we only pull more bytes earlier).
- Tie-breaking unchanged: N/A
- Floating-point: N/A
- RNG: N/A
- Golden outputs: cargo test -p ftui-tty ✓","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:36:20.281532639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T01:19:58.368337173Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:19:58.368319019Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:36:20.281532639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.2","title":"Implement: Unix raw mode + terminal feature toggles (alt-screen, mouse, paste, focus, kitty)","description":"Goal
- Replace Crossterm terminal mode management with our own, with stronger semantics and fewer surprises.

Requirements
- Raw mode enter/exit.
- Alt screen enter/leave.
- Mouse capture enable/disable.
- Bracketed paste + focus events.
- Kitty keyboard protocol enable/disable.
- Robust Drop-based cleanup + panic safety.

Acceptance Criteria
- PTY tests prove cleanup on normal exit and panic.
- No leaked terminal state (cursor visible, raw mode off, alt-screen restored).
